name: Agents Bundle
description: Mirrors skills-bundle for Claude Code subagent definitions. Three phase agents (cw-implementer, cw-reviewer, cw-fixer) are GENERATED from the shared phase procedures in packages/mcp-core/phase-prompts.cjs by packages/mcp-core/agents.cjs (indexed as BUNDLED_AGENTS, exposed via the "./agents" package export with .mjs/.d.ts shims), so agents, /cw-<phase> skills, and Phase Board prompts can never drift apart. The reviewer agent is code-read-only via a restricted tools frontmatter list; implementer/fixer inherit all tools. The extension's packages/extension/src/agentsBundle.ts installs them as <target>/.claude/agents/<name>.md (installWorkbenchAgents — a full reset, no subset option), byte-compares for drift (checkWorkbenchAgents), fingerprints the bundle (agentsBundleSignature), and removes unmodified copies (removeUnmodifiedWorkbenchAgents, used to retire the old user-scope backfill). PRIMARY distribution is now per-worktree session-launch injection via worktreeAssets.ts (see worktree-assets card) — the former backfillUserAgents user-scope auto-inject was REMOVED and its ~/.claude/agents leftovers are cleaned up one-time on activation. User/project scope installs remain available explicitly via the codeWorkbench.installWorkbenchAgents command, the global prefs panel (Agents section), or the per-worktree prefs panel.
anti_patterns: Do not reintroduce parallel-wave subagent dispatch into the phase procedures or agent bodies — each wave subagent re-explored the codebase from scratch and roughly doubled token usage. Phases run strictly sequentially in-session; phase agents must never spawn subagents.
decisions: 2026-07-13: Removed the order/parallel wave mechanic from all phase procedures (Plan no longer designs waves, Implement/Fix/Bulk no longer dispatch subagents) to cut token burn — ~50% of usage had come from wave subagents. The `parallel` task field remains in the data model as inert legacy metadata; `order` alone drives sequencing.
